title: ulmus campestris f. umbraculifera
intro: Ulmus campestris f. umbraculifera, also known as the umbrella elm, is a variety of the common elm tree (Ulmus campestris) that is characterized by its broad, rounded crown and umbrella-like shape. The bark of the umbrella elm has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the bark of the umbrella elm is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
